title: ICE detains Marlborough 14-year-old, apparently as ‘hostage’ to target father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rep. Lori Trahan claims ICE detained a 14-year-old Marlborough girl to coerce her father's surrender for deportation.
Trahan called for the teen's immediate release, criticizing the use of children in federal immigration enforcement.
Similar to the Liam Ramos case, the detainment has sparked outrage over the alleged targeting of minors by ICE.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Natalie Graves of Easthampton, Massachusetts, was among three women killed in a Utah multi-state triple homicide incident.
Graves, a dancer and cancer trials team member, was killed with her aunt while hiking near a Wayne County trailhead.
A GoFundMe campaign for Graves' husband has raised over $38,000 to aid in managing expenses and estate settlement.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Procter & Gamble announced a $1 billion investment for Gillette's new headquarters in Fort Point, South Boston.
The headquarters will be located at 232 A St. and includes a Grooming Headquarters and Technical Innovation Center.
Blade and razor manufacturing will move from South Boston to a new 200,000-square-foot facility in Andover.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Dre'Mont Jones joins the Patriots after signing a three-year deal; he previously played for the Ravens, Titans, Broncos, and Seahawks.
Jones had a standout 2025 season with career highs of seven sacks and 24 quarterback hits despite playing for two teams.
At 6'3" and 281 pounds, Jones offers versatility, capable of playing both on the edge and inside in a three-technique role.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor to close on March 29, citing economic challenges.
Closure affects 56 unionized workers, raising concerns of retaliation by UNITE HERE Local 26 union.
Seamark opened nearly two years ago, and workers voted 38-7 to unionize less than a month ago.

title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: St. Joseph School in Medford, founded in 1929, is closing due to declining enrollment and rising operational costs.
The school, serving pre-K through eighth grade, currently enrolls 169 students, down from 222 four years ago.
Efforts to find new Catholic school placements and offer tuition assistance are underway for impacted families and staff.

title: This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Chris Ruggio, 57, from Duxbury, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Children's Hospital Miles for Miracles team.
His daughter Holly, a patient at Boston Children's since a toddler, has juvenile idiopathic arthritis, a chronic condition.
Ruggio, a 14-time marathoner, has raised over $70,000 for children's health-focused charities through his marathon participation.

title: A 15-year-old girl was found dead in a Plymouth forest in 1986. Her alleged killer is finally standing trial.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Hand is on trial for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Myles Standish State Forest.
Hand was arrested in North Carolina in 2018, accused of killing Gilpin by dropping a 73-pound rock on her head.
Hand has pleaded not guilty; his defense argues his statements were coerced due to cognitive limitations and interrogation tactics.

title: Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds for each Mass. household
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey demands $4.9 billion in tariff refunds for Massachusetts, $1,745 per household, following Supreme Court ruling.
Supreme Court deemed Trump-era tariffs unconstitutional; Massachusetts residents reportedly paid extra due to increased costs of goods.
Healey urges the federal government to pay restitution and rethink new tariffs imposed by Trump under a different law.

title: Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Antonio Bonheur, 74, of Mattapan, pleaded guilty to $6.7 million fraudulent SNAP transactions at Jesula Variety Store.
Fraud involved converting SNAP benefits for cash and ineligible items, violating program rules over several years.
Co-accused Saul Alisme trafficked $121,000 in SNAP benefits; his case is postponed to mid-May.
